canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, social competence is a construct defined through several related lenses, including social awareness, social understanding, and humanistic care. The prevailing usage frames it as a latent quality for handling social situations appropriately, such as a conversational agent avoiding "socially deficient replies" ("Proceedings of the 2025 Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing"). Other definitions extend this to include the ability to handle "socially grounded judgments or commonsense social situations" ("Understanding and Leveraging the Expert Specialization of Context Faithfulness in Mixture-of-ExpertsLLMs") and to interpret "subtle, implicit social cues in human communication" beyond literal text ("Instructing Large Language Models for Low-Resource Languages: A Systematic Study forBasque"). A more specialized framing appears as "humanistic care," which focuses on respectful and empathetic communication in specific contexts. To operationalize this construct, the literature reports using the SocialIQA benchmark as a measurement instrument, suggesting that performance on commonsense social tasks is used as evidence for this capability.
papers: Dynamic Jointly Batch Selection for Data Efficient Machine Translation Fine-Tuning
Instructing Large Language Models for Low-Resource Languages: A Systematic Study forBasque
Proceedings of the 2025 Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
Understanding and Leveraging the Expert Specialization of Context Faithfulness in Mixture-of-ExpertsLLMs

definitions.text: The latent quality of a conversational agent's responses to behave appropriately in social interaction, including avoiding socially deficient replies.
definitions.paper: Proceedings of the 2025 Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing

definitions.text: The latent ability to handle socially grounded judgments or commonsense social situations in language tasks.
definitions.paper: Understanding and Leveraging the Expert Specialization of Context Faithfulness in Mixture-of-ExpertsLLMs

definitions.text: The ability to recognize and interpret subtle, implicit social cues in human communication, such as intentions and implications, beyond the literal meaning of text.
definitions.paper: Instructing Large Language Models for Low-Resource Languages: A Systematic Study forBasque

definitions.text: The tendency to communicate respectfully, empathetically, and politely while providing appropriate guidance and respecting patient wishes.
definitions.paper: Dynamic Jointly Batch Selection for Data Efficient Machine Translation Fine-Tuning

relationships.paper: Understanding and Leveraging the Expert Specialization of Context Faithfulness in Mixture-of-ExpertsLLMs

emnlp25: Understanding and Leveraging the Expert Specialization of Context Faithfulness in Mixture-of-ExpertsLLMs
